rulings.Case Title: LUCIANO COSLOVICH et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 29, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied, failure to shift the burden of proof under Weber because the record does not establish that plaintiff had a role in purchasing products. The Wade declaration establishes that Kelly-Moore products were on the job site. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LUCIANO COSLOVICH et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 29, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT T. H. AGRICULTURE & NUTRITION, L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: Hearing required. Defendant has shifted the burden of proof. Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses do not establish that plaintiff has any evidence to prove that asbestos sold by this defendant found its way into any of the products to which plaintiff was exposed. Plaintiff's request for a continuance fails to explain the delay in seeking the evidence and how it is the person most knowledgeable will be able to supply the evidence plaintiff needs. Plaintiff's objections to the Carter declaration are sustained for there is no foundation for his testimony before April 2002. However, his declaration is relevant when considering the request for a continuance. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LUCIANO COSLOVICH et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 29, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT KENTILE FLOORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied Defendant's separate statement fails to comply with CRC 3.1350(h) in that it does not highlight the material relied upon. Assuming defendant shifted the burden of proof, the Fervert declaration creates a triable issue of fact when coupled with defendant's acknowledgement that it has used asbestos in certain of its floor tiles. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: FARHAD S MAHJOURI VS. PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Severing This Action
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 29, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANTS PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY AND UNUM GROUP'S (F.K.A. UNUMPROVIDENT CORPORATION) Motion For An Order Severing This Action IS DENIED. THE COURT DOES NOT SEE THAT SEVERANCE WOULD ACHIEVE ANYTHING FOR THE FRESNO ACTION AND THIS ACTION COULD BE COORDINATED WITHOUT DOING SO. IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO AMEND ITS PLEADING TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER, THIS REQUEST WILL BE MOOT. =(302/PJM/PB)
